items.title: Compatibility and progressive enhancement: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling misconception? Adding every vendor prefix found online guarantees modern cross-browser compatibility.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. Use current support data and real matrix testing, add targeted fallbacks, and avoid obsolete cargo-cult prefixes.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. Use current support data and real matrix testing, add targeted fallbacks, and avoid obsolete cargo-cult prefixes.

criteria.description: Selects the source-linked CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling correction rather than the documented misconception.

options.text: Use current support data and real matrix testing, add targeted fallbacks, and avoid obsolete cargo-cult prefixes.

items.title: Compatibility and progressive enhancement: verified implementation facet
items.prompt: Check support and supply a usable baseline before optional newer features. Build a bounded artifact demonstrating usable flex fallback enhanced by @supports grid; the locked browser verifier checks observable structure, behavior, or computed presentation.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted authoring draft. Runtime evidence does not replace independent human standards, pedagogy, accessibility, answer-oracle, and formal-exam review.

hints: Implement the smallest artifact that satisfies the declared observable contract, then check the visible case before considering the hidden boundary.

feedback.correct: The locked authoring verifier observed the declared bounded facet in both visible and hidden cases.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic authoring check failed; inspect the exact artifact, interaction state, viewport, and observable result without assuming hidden-case details.

criteria.description: Passes the visible and hidden deterministic checks for the declared implementation facet in its pinned authoring environment.

privateAuthorNotes: This code item and its oracle are AI-assisted draft material with reviewer=null and examEligibility=false.
Browser-verifier items are authoring evidence only and must never be routed to the official untrusted-code runner or formal exam pipeline.
items.starterCode: /* TODO: implement the declared bounded CSS facet. */

answer.referenceSolution: .grid { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; } @supports (display: grid) { .grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(12rem, 1fr)); } }
answer.explanation: The reference is a bounded css artifact for usable flex fallback enhanced by @supports grid. Passing proves only the declared observable facet, not holistic mastery or human accessibility approval.
